text: Meteorology
the study of weather, atmosphere, and short-term changes in air conditions
original_text: Meteorology is the study of weather, atmosphere, and short-term changes in air conditions.

text: weather describes the current state of the atmosphere in a specific place
original_text: Weather describes the current state of the atmosphere in a specific place.

text: climate describes long-term patterns of weather over many years
original_text: Climate describes long-term patterns of weather over many years.

text: the atmosphere is the layer of gases surrounding Earth
original_text: The atmosphere is the layer of gases surrounding Earth.

text: most weather happens in the troposphere, the lowest layer of the atmosphere
original_text: Most weather happens in the troposphere, the lowest layer of the atmosphere.

text: air pressure is the force of air pressing on a surface
original_text: Air pressure is the force of air pressing on a surface.

text: high pressure often brings calmer, clearer weather
original_text: High pressure often brings calmer, clearer weather.

text: low pressure often brings clouds, precipitation, and unsettled weather
original_text: Low pressure often brings clouds, precipitation, and unsettled weather.

text: wind is moving air caused by pressure differences
original_text: Wind is moving air caused by pressure differences.

text: air moves from areas of higher pressure toward areas of lower pressure
original_text: Air moves from areas of higher pressure toward areas of lower pressure.

text: temperature measures how hot or cold the air is
original_text: Temperature measures how hot or cold the air is.

text: humidity measures how much water vapor is in the air
original_text: Humidity measures how much water vapor is in the air.

text: relative humidity compares current water vapor to the maximum amount air can hold at that temperature
original_text: Relative humidity compares current water vapor to the maximum amount air can hold at that temperature.

text: warm air can usually hold more water vapor than cold air
original_text: Warm air can usually hold more water vapor than cold air.

text: condensation happens when water vapor changes into liquid water
original_text: Condensation happens when water vapor changes into liquid water.

text: clouds form when moist air cools and water vapor condenses onto tiny particles
original_text: Clouds form when moist air cools and water vapor condenses onto tiny particles.

text: fog is basically a cloud near the ground
original_text: Fog is basically a cloud near the ground.

text: precipitation includes rain, snow, sleet, and hail
original_text: Precipitation includes rain, snow, sleet, and hail.

text: rain forms when cloud droplets grow heavy enough to fall
original_text: Rain forms when cloud droplets grow heavy enough to fall.

text: snow forms when water vapor freezes into ice crystals in cold clouds
original_text: Snow forms when water vapor freezes into ice crystals in cold clouds.

text: sleet forms when falling precipitation partially melts and then refreezes before reaching the ground
original_text: Sleet forms when falling precipitation partially melts and then refreezes before reaching the ground.

text: freezing rain falls as liquid but freezes when it touches cold surfaces
original_text: Freezing rain falls as liquid but freezes when it touches cold surfaces.

text: hail forms in strong thunderstorms with powerful updrafts
original_text: Hail forms in strong thunderstorms with powerful updrafts.

text: thunderstorms form when warm, moist air rises quickly and becomes unstable
original_text: Thunderstorms form when warm, moist air rises quickly and becomes unstable.

text: lightning is a sudden discharge of electricity in the atmosphere
original_text: Lightning is a sudden discharge of electricity in the atmosphere.

text: thunder is the sound produced by rapidly expanding air heated by lightning
original_text: Thunder is the sound produced by rapidly expanding air heated by lightning.

text: storm clouds can grow tall when the atmosphere is unstable
original_text: Storm clouds can grow tall when the atmosphere is unstable.

text: an updraft is rising air inside a storm
original_text: An updraft is rising air inside a storm.

text: A downdraft is sinking air inside a storm
original_text: A downdraft is sinking air inside a storm.

text: severe thunderstorms can produce damaging winds, hail, flash flooding, and tornadoes
original_text: Severe thunderstorms can produce damaging winds, hail, flash flooding, and tornadoes.

text: tornadoes are violently rotating columns of air connected to storms and the ground
original_text: Tornadoes are violently rotating columns of air connected to storms and the ground.

text: tornadoes often form in strong thunderstorms with wind shear
original_text: Tornadoes often form in strong thunderstorms with wind shear.

text: wind shear means wind changes speed or direction with height
original_text: Wind shear means wind changes speed or direction with height.

text: hurricanes are large rotating tropical storms that form over warm ocean water
original_text: Hurricanes are large rotating tropical storms that form over warm ocean water.

text: hurricanes need warm water, moist air, and favorable wind conditions
original_text: Hurricanes need warm water, moist air, and favorable wind conditions.

text: the eye of a hurricane is a calmer region near the center
original_text: The eye of a hurricane is a calmer region near the center.

text: the eyewall usually contains the strongest winds in a hurricane
original_text: The eyewall usually contains the strongest winds in a hurricane.

text: storm surge is ocean water pushed onto land by strong storm winds
original_text: Storm surge is ocean water pushed onto land by strong storm winds.

text: blizzards involve strong winds, falling or blowing snow, and low visibility
original_text: Blizzards involve strong winds, falling or blowing snow, and low visibility.

text: droughts happen when an area receives too little water for an extended time
original_text: Droughts happen when an area receives too little water for an extended time.

text: air masses are large bodies of air with similar temperature and humidity
original_text: Air masses are large bodies of air with similar temperature and humidity.

text: fronts are boundaries between air masses
original_text: Fronts are boundaries between air masses.

text: cold fronts occur when cold air pushes into warmer air
original_text: Cold fronts occur when cold air pushes into warmer air.

text: warm fronts occur when warm air moves over colder air
original_text: Warm fronts occur when warm air moves over colder air.

text: stationary fronts occur when air masses meet but neither advances much
original_text: Stationary fronts occur when air masses meet but neither advances much.

text: occluded fronts form when a cold front overtakes a warm front
original_text: Occluded fronts form when a cold front overtakes a warm front.

text: jet streams are fast-moving air currents high in the atmosphere
original_text: Jet streams are fast-moving air currents high in the atmosphere.

text: jet streams influence storm tracks and weather patterns
original_text: Jet streams influence storm tracks and weather patterns.

text: the Coriolis effect makes moving air curve because Earth rotates
original_text: The Coriolis effect makes moving air curve because Earth rotates.

text: earth's rotation helps shape global wind patterns
original_text: Earth's rotation helps shape global wind patterns.

text: convection happens when warmer, less dense air rises and cooler air sinks
original_text: Convection happens when warmer, less dense air rises and cooler air sinks.

text: convection is a major driver of clouds, storms, and atmospheric circulation
original_text: Convection is a major driver of clouds, storms, and atmospheric circulation.

text: radiation transfers energy through electromagnetic waves
original_text: Radiation transfers energy through electromagnetic waves.

text: conduction transfers heat through direct contact
original_text: Conduction transfers heat through direct contact.

text: the Sun provides most of the energy that drives Earth's weather
original_text: The Sun provides most of the energy that drives Earth's weather.

text: uneven heating of Earth's surface creates pressure differences and wind
original_text: Uneven heating of Earth's surface creates pressure differences and wind.

text: land heats and cools faster than water
original_text: Land heats and cools faster than water.

text: sea breezes form when cooler ocean air moves toward warmer land
original_text: Sea breezes form when cooler ocean air moves toward warmer land.

text: land breezes form when cooler land air moves toward warmer water at night
original_text: Land breezes form when cooler land air moves toward warmer water at night.

text: mountains can force air upward, causing cooling and cloud formation
original_text: Mountains can force air upward, causing cooling and cloud formation.

text: rain shadows form when mountains block moist air and create drier areas on the leeward side
original_text: Rain shadows form when mountains block moist air and create drier areas on the leeward side.

text: weather forecasts use observations, models, satellites, radar, and physics
original_text: Weather forecasts use observations, models, satellites, radar, and physics.

text: weather models simulate the atmosphere using mathematical equations
original_text: Weather models simulate the atmosphere using mathematical equations.

text: forecasts become less certain farther into the future
original_text: Forecasts become less certain farther into the future.

text: small changes in atmospheric conditions can affect later weather
original_text: Small changes in atmospheric conditions can affect later weather.

text: radar helps meteorologists track precipitation and storm movement
original_text: Radar helps meteorologists track precipitation and storm movement.

text: doppler radar can detect motion inside storms
original_text: Doppler radar can detect motion inside storms.

text: satellites observe clouds, storms, temperatures, water vapor, and large-scale patterns
original_text: Satellites observe clouds, storms, temperatures, water vapor, and large-scale patterns.

text: weather balloons measure temperature, humidity, pressure, and wind high in the atmosphere
original_text: Weather balloons measure temperature, humidity, pressure, and wind high in the atmosphere.

text: barometers measure air pressure
original_text: Barometers measure air pressure.

text: thermometers measure temperature
original_text: Thermometers measure temperature.

text: anemometers measure wind speed
original_text: Anemometers measure wind speed.

text: hygrometers measure humidity
original_text: Hygrometers measure humidity.

text: rain gauges measure precipitation
original_text: Rain gauges measure precipitation.

text: isobars are lines on weather maps connecting places with equal air pressure
original_text: Isobars are lines on weather maps connecting places with equal air pressure.

text: weather maps help show patterns in pressure, fronts, precipitation, and wind
original_text: Weather maps help show patterns in pressure, fronts, precipitation, and wind.

text: cloud type can provide clues about current and future weather
original_text: Cloud type can provide clues about current and future weather.

text: cumulus clouds are puffy clouds often associated with fair weather or developing storms
original_text: Cumulus clouds are puffy clouds often associated with fair weather or developing storms.

text: stratus clouds form flat layers and can bring gray, steady weather
original_text: Stratus clouds form flat layers and can bring gray, steady weather.

text: cirrus clouds are thin, high clouds made mostly of ice crystals
original_text: Cirrus clouds are thin, high clouds made mostly of ice crystals.

text: cumulonimbus clouds are tall storm clouds associated with thunderstorms
original_text: Cumulonimbus clouds are tall storm clouds associated with thunderstorms.

text: climate patterns influence what kinds of weather are common in a region
original_text: Climate patterns influence what kinds of weather are common in a region.

text: el Niño and La Niña can shift weather patterns around the world
original_text: El Niño and La Niña can shift weather patterns around the world.

text: helps people prepare for dangerous weather
original_text: Meteorology helps people prepare for dangerous weather.

text: forecast warnings can save lives when severe weather approaches
original_text: Forecast warnings can save lives when severe weather approaches.

text: weather affects transportation, agriculture, energy use, safety, and daily decisions
original_text: Weather affects transportation, agriculture, energy use, safety, and daily decisions.

text: meteorology combines observation, physics, chemistry, math, and computing
original_text: Meteorology combines observation, physics, chemistry, math, and computing.

text: the atmosphere is constantly moving, mixing, warming, cooling, rising, and sinking
original_text: The atmosphere is constantly moving, mixing, warming, cooling, rising, and sinking.

text: weather can seem random, but it follows physical laws
original_text: Weather can seem random, but it follows physical laws.

text: weather is hard to predict because the atmosphere is a chaotic system
original_text: Weather is hard to predict because the atmosphere is a chaotic system.

text: A sunny day and a thunderstorm both come from the same atmospheric rules
original_text: A sunny day and a thunderstorm both come from the same atmospheric rules.

text: turns clouds, wind, and pressure into readable patterns
original_text: Meteorology turns clouds, wind, and pressure into readable patterns.

text: weather is the atmosphere having a very public mood swing
original_text: Weather is the atmosphere having a very public mood swing.

text: explains why the sky changes its mind so often
original_text: Meteorology explains why the sky changes its mind so often.

text: the atmosphere is invisible until it starts making weather
original_text: The atmosphere is invisible until it starts making weather.

text: meteorology the science of sky sauce behavior
original_text: The meatball considers meteorology the science of sky sauce behavior.

text: cloud detective work with wind, pressure, and occasional chaos
original_text: Meteorology is cloud detective work with wind, pressure, and occasional chaos.

text: makes rain feel less random and more like physics with dramatic timing
original_text: Meteorology makes rain feel less random and more like physics with dramatic timing.

text: shows that the sky is complicated, moody, and mathematically suspicious
original_text: Meteorology shows that the sky is complicated, moody, and mathematically suspicious.

text: asks how the atmosphere moves, changes, and turns invisible forces into weather
original_text: At its core, meteorology asks how the atmosphere moves, changes, and turns invisible forces into weather.
